title: Gilia mohavensis
taxonomy: Gilia mohavensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Gilia, which consists of various species of annuals and perennials. The Polemoniaceae family, to which Gilia mohavensis belongs, comprises approximately 15 genera and over 300 species. Gilia mohavensis is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, where it grows in dry, sandy areas. As a member of the Polemoniaceae family, Gilia mohavensis exhibits characteristics typical of this family, such as its five-petaled flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves.

intro: Gilia mohavensis, also known as Mojave gilia, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Gilia mohavensis include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
